briefdescription.para: encodes a point inside a triangle using barycentric coordinates

detaileddescription.para: Notations used below: v0, v1, v2 - points of the triangle Generated from class MR::TriPointf. Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

briefdescription.para: encodes a point inside a triangle using barycentric coordinates

detaileddescription.para: Notations used below: v0, v1, v2 - points of the triangle Generated from class MR::TriPointd. Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

Returns a pointer to a member variable of class MR::TriPointf named eps. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. a in [0,1], a=0 => point is on [v2,v0] edge, a=1 => point is in v1 Returns a pointer to a member variable of class MR::TriPointf named a. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.

a in [0,1], a=0 => point is on [v2,v0] edge, a=1 => point is in v1 Modifies a member variable of class MR::TriPointf named a. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. When this function is called, this object will drop object references it had previously in a.

a in [0,1], a=0 => point is on [v2,v0] edge, a=1 => point is in v1 Returns a mutable pointer to a member variable of class MR::TriPointf named a. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.

b in [0,1], b=0 => point is on [v0,v1] edge, b=1 => point is in v2 Returns a pointer to a member variable of class MR::TriPointf named b. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.

b in [0,1], b=0 => point is on [v0,v1] edge, b=1 => point is in v2 Modifies a member variable of class MR::TriPointf named b. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. When this function is called, this object will drop object references it had previously in b.

b in [0,1], b=0 => point is on [v0,v1] edge, b=1 => point is in v2 Returns a mutable pointer to a member variable of class MR::TriPointf named b. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.
Constructs an empty (default-constructed) instance. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_TriPointf_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Constructs an array of empty (default-constructed) instances, of the specified size. Will never return null. The array must be destroyed using MR_TriPointf_DestroyArray(). Use MR_TriPointf_OffsetMutablePtr() and MR_TriPointf_OffsetPtr() to access the array elements.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Generated from constructor MR::TriPointf::TriPointf. Parameter _other can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to things referred to by the parameter _other (if any) might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_TriPointf_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Generated from constructor MR::TriPointf::TriPointf. Parameter _1 can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to the parameter _1 might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_TriPointf_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.
Generated from constructor MR::TriPointf::TriPointf. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_TriPointf_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MR_Vector3f *p, const MR_Vector3f *v0, const MR_Vector3f *v1, const MR_Vector3f *v2)
memberdef.name: MR_TriPointf_Construct_4

detaileddescription.para: given a point coordinates and triangle (v0,v1,v2) computes barycentric coordinates of the point Generated from constructor MR::TriPointf::TriPointf. Parameter p can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to the parameter p might be preserved in the constructed object. Parameter v0 can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to the parameter v0 might be preserved in the constructed object. Parameter v1 can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to the parameter v1 might be preserved in the constructed object. Parameter v2 can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to the parameter v2 might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_TriPointf_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

given a point coordinates and triangle (0,v1,v2) computes barycentric coordinates of the point Generated from constructor MR::TriPointf::TriPointf. Parameter p can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to the parameter p might be preserved in the constructed object. Parameter v1 can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to the parameter v1 might be preserved in the constructed object. Parameter v2 can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to the parameter v2 might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_TriPointf_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

briefdescription.para: Destroys a heap-allocated instance of MR_TriPointf. Does nothing if the pointer is null.
Destroys a heap-allocated array of MR_TriPointf. Does nothing if the pointer is null.

detaileddescription.para: Generated from method MR::TriPointf::operator=. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter _other can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to things referred to by the parameter _other (if any) might be preserved in this object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. When this function is called, this object will drop any object references it had previously.

represents the same point relative to next edge in the same triangle Generated from method MR::TriPointf::lnext. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_TriPointf_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.
returns [0,2] if the point is in a vertex or -1 otherwise Generated from method MR::TriPointf::inVertex. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.

returns [0,2] if the point is on edge or -1 otherwise: 0 means edge [v1,v2]; 1 means edge [v2,v0]; 2 means edge [v0,v1] Generated from method MR::TriPointf::onEdge. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.

returns true if two points have equal (a,b) representation Generated from method MR::TriPointf::operator==. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter rhs can not be null. It is a single object.

Generated from function MR::operator<<. Parameter s can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter tp can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.

Generated from function MR::operator>>. Parameter s can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter tp can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.

Returns a pointer to a member variable of class MR::TriPointd named eps. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. a in [0,1], a=0 => point is on [v2,v0] edge, a=1 => point is in v1 Returns a pointer to a member variable of class MR::TriPointd named a.
